keyPointsSummary: 'Get someone to do something' = persuade them (with 'to')
'Get something done' = causative, someone else does it for you
'Make' also means to create/produce; 'Get' also means to obtain/become

quickQuiz.hindi: मैंने उसे यह काम करने पर मजबूर किया।
quickQuiz.answer: I made him do this work.

quickQuiz.hindi: मैंने उसे यह काम करने के लिए मनाया।
quickQuiz.answer: I got him to do this work.

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं अपनी कार ठीक करवाता हूँ।
उसने मुझे हंसने पर मजबूर कर दिया।
मैंने उसे मानने के लिए राज़ी किया।
टीचर ने हमें चुप रहने को कहा (मजबूर किया)।
quickQuiz.answer: The teacher made us stay quiet.

quickQuiz.hindi: मैं अपना homework पूरा करवाता हूँ।

mustRememberRules: 'Get someone to do something' = persuade them (with 'to')
'Get something done' = causative, someone else does it for you
'Make' also means to create/produce; 'Get' also means to obtain/become
